questions.question: A cube has surface area 600 cm². Find its volume.
questions.answer: SA = 6a² → a² = 600/6 = 100 → a = 10 cm; Volume = a³ = 10³ = 1000 cm³

questions.question: A sphere has surface area 154 cm². Find its radius and volume.
The height of a cylinder is 10 cm and its curved surface area is 220 cm². Find its radius.
A cone has slant height 13 cm and radius 5 cm. Find its curved surface area and total surface area.
A cylinder and a cone have the same base radius 7 cm and same height 24 cm. Find the ratio of their volumes.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π×49×24 = 1176π, Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h = 392π; Ratio = 1176π:392π = 3:1

questions.question: A sphere has radius 14 cm. A hemisphere is cut out of it. Find the remaining surface area.
questions.answer: Surface area of sphere = 4πr² = 4π×14² = 784π; Hemisphere removed exposes flat circle area = πr² = 196π; Remaining TSA = 784π - 196π + 196π = 784π ≈ 2462.4 cm²

questions.question: A metallic sheet is used to make 10 identical spheres of radius 3 cm each. Find the total surface area of the sheet required.
questions.answer: Surface area of one sphere = 4πr² = 4π×9 = 36π; Total for 10 spheres = 10×36π = 360π ≈ 1130.97 cm²

questions.question: A cylindrical container has radius 14 cm and height 30 cm. Find its total surface area and volume.
questions.answer: CSA = 2πrh = 2π×14×30 = 840π, TSA = 2πr(h+r) = 2π×14×44 = 1232π, Volume = πr²h = π×196×30 = 5880π; CSA≈2637.1 cm², TSA≈3870.4 cm², Volume≈18458.5 cm³

questions.question: A cone has base radius 7 cm and volume 154 cm³. Find its height.
A sphere is melted and recast into smaller spheres each of radius 2 cm. If the original sphere had radius 14 cm, find the number of smaller spheres.
questions.answer: Volume of original = 4/3π×14³ = 11494.7 cm³, Volume of smaller = 4/3π×2³ = 33.51 cm³, Number = 11494.7/33.51 ≈ 343 spheres

questions.question: A cube of side 12 cm is melted and recast into cuboids of dimensions 4 cm × 3 cm × 2 cm. Find the number of cuboids.
questions.answer: Volume of cube = 12³ = 1728 cm³, Volume of cuboid = 4×3×2 = 24 cm³, Number = 1728/24 = 72 cuboids

questions.question: A cone and a cylinder have same base radius and height. Compare their surface areas.
questions.answer: CSA cone = πrl < CSA cylinder = 2πrh, TSA cone = πr(l+r), TSA cylinder = 2πr(h+r); Generally, CSA cone < CSA cylinder, TSA depends on slant height

questions.question: Find the volume of a frustum of a cone with radii 7 cm, 3 cm and height 8 cm.
A solid cylinder of radius 7 cm and height 15 cm is melted to make cones of radius 7 cm and height 5 cm. Find number of cones.
questions.answer: Volume cylinder = πr²h = π×49×15 = 735π, Volume one cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π×49×5 = 81.667π, Number = 735/81.667 ≈ 9 cones

questions.question: A sphere of radius 6 cm is cut into two equal hemispheres. Find the total surface area of both hemispheres.
questions.answer: CSA hemisphere = 2πr², TSA hemisphere = 3πr²; Total SA = 2×3π6² = 216π ≈ 678.6 cm²

questions.question: A solid hemisphere of radius 14 cm is melted to make smaller spheres each of radius 7 cm. Find the number of spheres.
questions.answer: Volume hemisphere = (2/3)π14³ = 1146.67π, Volume smaller sphere = (4/3)π7³ = 1436.0π, Number = 11466/1436 ≈ 2 spheres

questions.question: A metallic sphere of radius 21 cm is melted to form cylindrical rods of radius 3 cm and height 7 cm. Find the number of rods.
questions.answer: Volume sphere = (4/3)π×9261 = 12348π, Volume rod = π×9×7 = 63π, Number = 12348/63 ≈ 196 rods

questions.question: A cone has base radius 14 cm and volume 3080 cm³. Find its height.
A sphere and a cube have the same volume. Find the ratio of their surface areas.
questions.answer: Let volume = V; Sphere radius r = (3V/4π)^(1/3), Surface area = 4πr²; Cube side a = V^(1/3), Surface area = 6a²; Ratio SA_sphere : SA_cube < 1, sphere has smaller surface area

questions.question: A cylinder of height 15 cm and radius 7 cm is melted to make cones of height 10 cm and same base radius. Find number of cones.
questions.answer: Volume cylinder = πr²h = π×49×15 = 735π, Volume cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π×49×10 = 163.33π, Number = 735/163.33 ≈ 4 cones

questions.question: A solid hemisphere of radius 21 cm is melted to form small spheres each of radius 7 cm. Find the number of spheres.
questions.answer: Volume hemisphere = (2/3)π21³ = 6174π, Volume smaller sphere = (4/3)π7³ = 1436π, Number = 6174/1436 ≈ 4 spheres

questions.question: A cube has side 12 cm. A smaller cube of side 6 cm is cut from it. Find the remaining surface area.
questions.answer: Original SA = 6×12²=864 cm²; Small cube cut exposes 3 faces = 6×6=216 cm²; Remaining SA = 864-36 (overlap) + 216 = 1044 cm²

questions.question: A cylinder has diameter 14 cm and height 20 cm. Find its volume and total surface area.
A cone has base radius 10 cm and height 24 cm. Find its slant height, CSA and TSA.
A sphere of radius 21 cm is melted to form cylinders of radius 7 cm and height 9 cm. Find the number of cylinders.
questions.answer: Volume sphere = 4/3π21³ = 12348π, Volume cylinder = π7²×9 = 441π, Number = 12348/441 ≈ 28 cylinders
